# Dibbayajyoti Roy — Personal Portfolio

> Full Stack Software Engineer specializing in TypeScript, Rust, and Linux Infrastructure. Based in Agartala, Tripura, India. Creator of Klinder-OSS, Diffcore (npm), and the AHTML (Agentic HTML) shipped nine-package npm scope (@ahtmljs).

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://dibbayajyoti.com): Personal landing page with interactive hero section showcasing engineering skills and approach.
- [Work](https://dibbayajyoti.com/work): Full professional profile — experience, projects, technical skills, hackathon wins, and career philosophy.
- [Writing](https://dibbayajyoti.com/writing): Long-form engineering articles on production incidents, AI engineering, and modern web performance.
- [Rust Engineering](https://dibbayajyoti.com/rust): Rust backend engineering, Rust + WebAssembly, workers-rs on Cloudflare Workers, async API development, and systems programming.
- [WebAssembly](https://dibbayajyoti.com/webassembly): WebAssembly engineering — Rust compiled to WASM and shipped to npm, anchored by the Diffcore JSON diff engine.
- [AI Engineering](https://dibbayajyoti.com/ai-engineering): AWS Bedrock applications, multi-model fallback, RAG architecture, the MCP protocol, LangChain.js, and AI crawler optimization.
- [Next.js SEO](https://dibbayajyoti.com/nextjs-seo): Next.js Metadata API, JSON-LD structured data, sitemap optimization, llms.txt for AI crawlers, and Core Web Vitals.
- [Distributed Systems](https://dibbayajyoti.com/distributed-systems): Edge ingestion on Cloudflare Workers, Redis polling optimization, multi-tenant Postgres with RLS, and backend scaling.
- [Open Source](https://dibbayajyoti.com/open-source): Merged upstream contributions — ReductStore (Rust time-series database) replication audit events, PR #1417, shipping in v1.20, publicly endorsed by the project's co-founder.

## About

Dibbayajyoti Roy is a Full Stack Software Engineer currently working at Yupcha Softwares Pvt. Ltd (since July 2025). He builds production-grade SaaS platforms, deploys Node.js and Next.js services to Proxmox-hosted Ubuntu VMs with nginx, systemd, and TLS via Let's Encrypt, and designs REST APIs with PostgreSQL.

He is the creator of Klinder-OSS — an open-source SDK that unifies event tracking, error-based session recording, and automatic email trigger workflows in a single install. He also built diffcore, a fast WebAssembly JSON diff engine on npm written in Rust — returns real JSON Pointer paths, emits RFC 6902 JSON Patch, ships applyPatch / revertPatch, a React useDiff hook, a CLI, and a streaming engine; benchmarked 2.3–3.0× faster than fast-json-patch and 4–8× faster than jsondiffpatch. He also shipped AHTML — a nine-package npm scope at `@ahtmljs/*` (v0.9.5) covering the canonical agent snapshot schema (`@ahtmljs/schema`), Next.js, Vite, and Hono adapters that emit MCP/OpenAPI/JSON-LD/llms.txt + the AHTML snapshot at `/.well-known/ahtml.json` (`@ahtmljs/next`, `@ahtmljs/vite`, `@ahtmljs/hono`), pluggable cache/KV backends (`@ahtmljs/kv`), a W3C WebMCP bridge (`@ahtmljs/webmcp`), a client SDK for AI agents with dry-run safety gates (`@ahtmljs/agent`), a LangChain.js loader with chunk-boundary citation metadata (`@ahtmljs/langchain`), and an `ahtml doctor` discovery-chain linter CLI (`@ahtmljs/cli`).

He also shipped Learning Copilot, an AWS Bedrock-powered AI learning assistant that placed top 500 in the AI for Bharat hackathon.

B.Tech Computer Science candidate at ICFAI University Tripura (Class of 2026). Eligible for Germany Opportunity Card / EU Blue Card and Netherlands Highly Skilled Migrant programme.

## Technical Skills

- **Languages**: TypeScript, JavaScript (ES2023), Rust, SQL, Python
- **Frontend**: React, Next.js, TanStack Query, Tailwind CSS
- **Backend**: Node.js, Hono, Express, REST APIs, Server-Sent Events, Zod, OAuth, JWT
- **Systems / WASM**: Rust, WebAssembly, workers-rs
- **Infrastructure**: Linux, Proxmox VE, nginx, systemd, Docker, Cloudflare Workers, Cloudflare Queues, AWS (Bedrock, DynamoDB, CloudWatch)
- **Databases**: PostgreSQL (Neon, RLS, partitioning), DynamoDB, MongoDB, Redis
- **AI/ML**: AWS Bedrock (Nova Pro, Nova Lite), multi-model orchestration, streaming inference, agent design

## Projects

- **Klinder-OSS** (2026): Open-source SDK that unifies event tracking, error-based session recording, and automatic email trigger workflows in one install. Typed events with Zod, edge ingestion on Cloudflare Workers + Queues, Neon Postgres with RLS for multi-tenant isolation, Hono API layer. Rust port via workers-rs in progress targeting sub-10ms p95 latency. Live: https://klinder-oss.vercel.app
- **diffcore** (2026, v1.2.0): Fast WebAssembly JSON diff engine for JavaScript and TypeScript, written in Rust. Returns real JSON Pointer paths (RFC 6901) and decoded values, emits standard RFC 6902 JSON Patch, and ships `applyPatch` / `revertPatch` for state sync, undo/redo, and optimistic UI. Includes a React `useDiff` hook, a CLI, and a streaming engine for multi-GB files. 2.3–3.0× faster than fast-json-patch and 4–8× faster than jsondiffpatch (390–650 MB/s sustained). Runs on Node, browsers, Bun, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge, Electron, Tauri. npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/diffcore — demo: https://rust-wasm-library.vercel.app — repo: https://github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/rust-wasm-Library
- **AHTML — @ahtmljs npm scope** (shipped, 2026, v0.9.5): A nine-package npm scope for making web content cheaply consumable by AI agents. Repo: https://github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/AHTML — npm scope: https://www.npmjs.com/org/ahtmljs
  - **@ahtmljs/schema** — canonical semantic snapshot schema; TS types + JSON Schema + validator + dual serializers (lossless JSON / token-optimal compact text); ships `Document.chunks` for RAG with deterministic IDs and byte ranges. 177 tests.
  - **@ahtmljs/next** — Next.js plugin emitting MCP, OpenAPI, JSON-LD, llms.txt, and the AHTML snapshot at `/.well-known/ahtml.json`. Zero-migration; auto-discovers App/Pages Router routes. 53 tests.
  - **@ahtmljs/agent** — client SDK for AI agents consuming AHTML sites; ETag caching, dry-run safety gates, hostile-agent regression tests, tokenizer adapters (OpenAI o200k_base, Anthropic) for exact prompt cost. 57 tests.
  - **@ahtmljs/vite** — Vite plugin counterpart; byte-identical output to @ahtmljs/next; works on SvelteKit, SolidStart, Astro, vanilla Vite. 14 tests.
  - **@ahtmljs/langchain** — LangChain.js document loader; URL → embeddings in 3 lines, preserves citation anchors + byte ranges + source URLs. 5 tests.
  - **@ahtmljs/hono** — Hono adapter; same well-known endpoints as the Next.js and Vite plugins, running on Node, Bun, Deno, and Cloudflare Workers. 18 tests.
  - **@ahtmljs/kv** — pluggable KV and cache backends supporting Memory, Upstash Redis, and Cloudflare KV.
  - **@ahtmljs/webmcp** — W3C WebMCP bridge for exposing page actions as browser tools.
  - **@ahtmljs/cli** — `ahtml doctor`; a discovery-chain linter for CI that validates a site's MCP / OpenAPI / JSON-LD / llms.txt / AHTML snapshot wiring. 9 tests.
- **Learning Copilot** (2026): AWS Bedrock AI learning assistant with Nova Pro / Nova Lite multi-model fallback, structured level-adaptive explanations, auto-generated D2 diagrams, streaming inference. Top 500, AI for Bharat hackathon. Live: https://ai-for-bharat.vercel.app
- **BloodLink** (2024): Blood donor-seeker matching platform. Next.js + PostgreSQL geolocation. ~100 pilot users. https://github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/BloodLink
- **EMS**: Real-time Employee Management System in Rust and React. https://github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/Rust-FullStack-App

## Honors & Awards

- Winner — NITA Arjuna 2.0 National Hackathon (2025), 200+ teams
- Winner — Technovate Project Exhibition (2025)
- 1st Runner-Up — NITA–ISRO Space Hackathon (2024)
- Top 500 — AI for Bharat Hackathon (2026)

## FAQ

- **Who is Dibbayajyoti Roy?** Full Stack Software Engineer at Yupcha Softwares Pvt. Ltd, Agartala, India. Creator of Klinder-OSS, Diffcore, and AHTML (shipped nine-package @ahtmljs npm scope). Specializes in TypeScript, Rust, React, Next.js, AWS Bedrock.
- **What is Klinder-OSS?** An open-source SDK that unifies event tracking, error-based session recording, and automatic email trigger workflows in one install. Cloudflare Workers + Queues for ingestion, Neon Postgres with RLS for multi-tenancy, TypeScript SDK with Zod validation, Rust port in progress.
- **What is diffcore?** A fast WebAssembly JSON diff engine by Dibbayajyoti Roy, written in Rust and published on npm. Returns real JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) paths and decoded values, emits standard RFC 6902 JSON Patch, ships applyPatch / revertPatch for state sync, undo/redo, and optimistic UI, plus a React useDiff hook, a CLI, and a streaming engine for multi-GB files. Benchmarks 2.3–3.0× faster than fast-json-patch and 4–8× faster than jsondiffpatch (390–650 MB/s sustained).
- **What is AHTML?** AHTML (Agentic HTML) is a shipped nine-package npm scope (`@ahtmljs/*` at v0.9.5) by Dibbayajyoti Roy for making web content cheaply consumable by AI agents. Includes `@ahtmljs/schema` (canonical snapshot + RAG-ready `Document.chunks`, 177 tests), `@ahtmljs/next` (Next.js plugin emitting MCP/OpenAPI/JSON-LD/llms.txt/snapshot at `/.well-known/ahtml.json`, 53 tests), `@ahtmljs/agent` (client SDK with ETag caching + dry-run gates + hostile-agent regressions, 57 tests), `@ahtmljs/vite` (Vite plugin counterpart, byte-identical output, 14 tests), `@ahtmljs/langchain` (LangChain.js loader with citation metadata, 5 tests), `@ahtmljs/hono` (Hono adapter, 18 tests), `@ahtmljs/kv` (pluggable KV and cache backends), `@ahtmljs/webmcp` (WebMCP tool registry & browser bookmarklet for page actions), and `@ahtmljs/cli` (`ahtml doctor` discovery-chain linter for CI, 9 tests). Repo: github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy/AHTML.
- **Does Dibbayajyoti Roy have AI engineering experience?** Yes — built Learning Copilot using AWS Bedrock (Nova Pro / Nova Lite), multi-model fallback, streaming inference, DynamoDB memory, cost-aware token budgets. Top 500 AI for Bharat hackathon.
- **What hackathons has Dibbayajyoti Roy won?** NITA Arjuna 2.0 National Hackathon (2025, 200+ teams), Technovate Project Exhibition (2025), 1st Runner-Up NITA–ISRO Space Hackathon (2024), Top 500 AI for Bharat (2026).
- **Is Dibbayajyoti Roy eligible to work in Europe?** Yes — Germany Opportunity Card / EU Blue Card and Netherlands HSM.
- **Where does Dibbayajyoti Roy work?** Yupcha Softwares Pvt. Ltd, Full Stack Software Engineer, since July 2025, Agartala, India.
- **How to contact Dibbayajyoti Roy?** Email: dibbayajyoti@gmail.com, LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/dibbayajyoti-roy/, GitHub: github.com/DibbayajyotiRoy.

## Writing

- [Getting Code Merged Into Meta's Pyrefly and ReductStore](https://dibbayajyoti.com/writing/merged-into-pyrefly-and-reductstore) (2026): How Dibbayajyoti Roy landed merged Rust contributions in Meta's Pyrefly type checker (PR #3840) and ReductStore (PRs #1417, #1431) — reproduce before claiming, pick deterministic bugs, work with maintainers. Also on Medium.
- [Optimistic UIs in React with RFC 6902 JSON Patch](https://dibbayajyoti.com/writing/optimistic-ui-rfc-6902-react) (2026): One diff primitive for optimistic apply, rollback on failure, and undo/redo in React — using RFC 6902 JSON Patch and the diffcore Rust/WASM engine.
- [Next.js SEO in 2026: A Practical Guide](https://dibbayajyoti.com/writing/nextjs-seo-2026) (2026): The Metadata API, JSON-LD entity graphs, llms.txt for AI crawlers, honest sitemaps, and Core Web Vitals (INP) for Next.js sites. Also on Medium.
- [Fixing a Redis Polling Bottleneck in Production](https://dibbayajyoti.com/writing/redis-polling-bottleneck) (2026): A naive SCAN-and-check loop timed out at 100s; batched MGET with client-side filtering cut the work ~90% and ended the timeouts. Also on Medium.
